sections.heading: 1. What is a Coroutine?
sections.content: A coroutine is a lightweight thread. Unlike real threads, coroutines are inexpensive and you can run thousands of them on a single thread. They allow asynchronous programming without blocking the UI thread.

sections.heading: Key Concept: Suspend vs Blocking
items: Blocking: A thread waits for a task to finish and cannot do other work (bad for UI).
Suspending: A coroutine pauses without blocking the thread, allowing other work to run. It resumes when the task completes.

sections.heading: 2A. suspend Functions
sections.content: Functions marked with 'suspend' can be paused and resumed. They can only be called from another suspend function or inside a coroutine.

sections.heading: 2B. Coroutine Scope
sections.content: A coroutine scope defines the lifecycle of coroutines. In Android, scopes like lifecycleScope ensure coroutines are cancelled automatically when the Activity or Fragment is destroyed.

sections.heading: 2C. Coroutine Builders
sections.content: Coroutine builders are used to start coroutines.

sections.heading: Common Builders
items: launch: Starts a coroutine and does not return a result (fire-and-forget).
async: Starts a coroutine and returns Deferred<T>, allowing you to retrieve a result using await().

sections.heading: 4. Simple Coroutine Example (Android)
sections.content: This example shows how to perform background work and then switch back to the Main thread to update the UI.

sections.heading: 5. Structured Concurrency
sections.content: Coroutines follow a hierarchy. If a parent coroutine is cancelled, all of its child coroutines are cancelled automatically. This prevents background tasks from leaking or continuing after the UI is destroyed.

sections.heading: Summary
items: Coroutines are lightweight and do not block threads.
The 'suspend' keyword allows functions to pause without blocking.
launch starts a coroutine; async returns a value.
Dispatchers determine which thread the coroutine runs on.
Structured concurrency prevents memory leaks in Android.
